title: Finished AC Chemo, onto Taxol in 2 weeks *** Tips for managing side effects on AC***
body:
This post is for anyone about to start AC a.k.a The Red Devil treatment. Here are some tips that helped me manage the fatigue and nausea:

- Drink water! Drink more water! Just join the ocean world already! That is how much water you will need. AC dries you out. 
-I didnt cold cap, so I cut my hair in advance to avoid the traumatic effects of losing my hair. Interestingly enough, before diagnosis, I wore wigs as a choice. With chemo, there is no choice, so I stopped wearing wigs and embraced my Dole Meljea look (Wakanda Forever). Life's outlook can be funny that way.
-Ask for hydration and see if your insurance covers it. Got Neulasta after each treatment. Greatly helped me stay focused. No bone pain. Took Claritin before, on, and after chemo days. 
-Working Full-Time (remotely)
-Take it slow! I mean really slow! Your heart rate may be pounding faster than ever on AC! 
- Biotene mouthwash and toothpaste are highy recommended for mouth sores. Never got any.
- Take your anti-nausea drugs as directed by your doctors. On other days, consume bananas, unsweetened applesauce, and oatmeal. They are your best friends for nausea.
- Eat as healthy as you can. Berries, cruciferous veggies,melons, tomatoes etc. I followed the scientific studies on foodforbreastcancers.com, which helped me tailored my nutrition around my chemo. Ate foods that boost the effectiveness of AC chemo. I went into chemo very healthy but I think what I ate during AC made a huge difference. My lump decreased from 5 cm to 2 cm after the 3rd AC injection. Waiting to see how much more it decreases by after the last one, this past Thursday. Wont know until end of Jan.
- Pedialyte tasted nasty, so I drank coconut water for electrolytes. Stuck to clear water and smoothies mostly.
- Don't be afraid to set boundaries with people sending you cures about your cancer. All of a sudden, everyone has a PHD in your circle. Tell them nicely or nastily to give you space to be human.


I did well on AC. Some manageable fatigue. My hands did get darker (I am already dark skin) but this is temporary. This is a confusing time for many of us. Nonetheless, we will persevere. Hope this post helps someone!
